title: InfraContainer Rework
body:
InfraContainer should go through the same creation process as regular containers. This change was from the cmd level
down, involving new container CLI opts and specgen creating functions. What now happens is that both container and pod
cli options are populated in cmd and used to create a podSpecgen and a containerSpecgen. The process then goes as follows

**FillOutSpecGen (infra)  -> PodCreate -> MakePod ->  MapSpec (podSpec -> infraSpec) -> createPodOptions -> NewPod -> CompleteSpec (infra) -> MakeContainer -> ExecuteCreate -> NewContainer -> newContainer -> AddInfra (to pod state)**

removed runtime_pod_infra_linux.go, infra now is on its way to following basic container creation path.

resolves #10933

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: @umohnani8 @rhatdan @mheon PTAL, cross tests are failing which I can't figure out why, but besides that the general structure of this is ready for review. I am sure it will be ripped apart because I changed a lot but the general idea stands: infra is now created via container CLI options and the specgen process from the top of the call stack. Adding new pod create options should be as easy as moving a flag declaration out of `!isInfra` in `common.DefineCreateFlags`

I'll do a full review tomorrow morning. The cross-build failures are caused by OS-specific differences - it looks like you're using a struct member that exists on Linux, but one on OS X and Windows. Generally we have to do OS-specific compilation (usually a function defined separately for Linux and OS X / Windows that reads the bit you need) to work around this.

@mheon I figured out the cross failures, it was due to calling `/libpod/network` within `pkg/specgenutils`. I have a new push with a few nits and cleanups, but want the test to pass first so if you review and see some leftover code or code without comments, a fix is incoming soon!

@mheon I made a bunch of your changes and will continue when I am back. I also want to talk about some of the changes regarding the `With...` functions and the resource limits. Thanks for the feedback and all of your help on my pod create PRs so far
Someone else should review the CLI bits of this (maybe @jwhonce or @vrothberg) - I'm not as familiar with those bits
@cdoern No problem, pleasure working with you!
@mheon PTAL
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@vrothberg PTAL

just for some context @vrothberg, what I did here was basically pull out the old infra creation codepath and line it up (as best as I could) with the regular container specgen and creation process. This will allow for easy implementation of new pod create options as most of them are container create options applied to infra manually. Now they won't need to be manual anymore but rather will happen as they do with a regular container. Only thing I am wondering about is some image stuff (pulling the pause image manually each time, should we make a function?) and want someone to take a look at the libpod api handler for pod create since I really redesigned that to use Container CLI Options and Pod Create Options rather than Specgen (again, allows for more feature parity down the line)
Thanks for the pings!  I'll review tomorrow with a fresh pair of eyes.

Ok @rhatdan I have made all of the changes from reviews and tests seem to be happy. If there are no other concerns I would like to get this in before having to rebase again since this basically touches all major aspects of podman. With this merged I will be able to get on my way with the pod create options!
@mheon @Luap99 just had an idea for the libpod endpoint regarding the netflags, let me see if it works I will push it up to here if it does.

If I understand this correctly then the pod create endpoint should accept a podspecgen and the podspecgen has a containerspecgen field to customise the infra container. All settings which can only be used with the infra container (e.g the network settings) should be set in the containerspecgen. The podspecgen should only contain fields which do not require a infra container.

> If I understand this correctly then the pod create endpoint should accept a podspecgen and the podspecgen has a containerspecgen field to customise the infra container. All settings which can only be used with the infra container (e.g the network settings) should be set in the containerspecgen. The podspecgen should only contain fields which do not require a infra container.

I do not think going through specgen initially makes sense with my approach. One of the main points of this whole change is to eventually get to specgen but line up the creation process nicely so that features can be easily added down the line. Locking this process into podspec leaves less room for change since it is arbitrarily down the stack as compared to the CLI options. I think starting with podcreateoptions and containercreateoptions makes the most sense. 

I am also not a fan of putting a containercreateoptions inside of podcreateoptions, these processes should be linked but not too heavily connected.

Infra's spec needs to be more manually groomed than just plopping the unmarshalled JSON into it, that is why i now use the `FillOutSpecGen` function here

Tend to agree with @Luap99 - we can't make the CLI flags part of the HTTP API. We don't even control the structure - I don't think anyone makes guarantees that flags will convert sanely to and from JSON, so upgrades to the CLI library could break this at any point without breaking their compatibility promise.

The proposal of including a container Specgen in `PodSpecGen` for the infra container sounds like the best option at present.

Ok @mheon @Luap99, trying that approach now. Might still have to do some manual poking at infra's spec to make sure its all good after decoding but I think this can work with my approach, thanks for all of the input

@mheon any ideas why the unmarshalling isn't happening properly on the remote integration tests? This is what was happening before I made the switch to ContainerCreateOptions/PodCreateOptions

Edit: found the issue in `CreatePodFromSpec`

@mheon, libpod API now decodes into a `podspecgen` which contains a container `specgen`, this allowed for removal of the `MapOpts` function and some other extraneous lines/functions I wrote. Tests seem to be happy, let me know if it is looking good! API handler stuff was really the only thing I was concerned about, thanks for the help on that.
Also I recommend to wait with merging this until we have a 3.4 branch. /cc @mheon

> Also I recommend to wait with merging this until we have a 3.4 branch. /cc @mheon 

I would agree except for the fact that each rebase I get from someone else's feature runs the risk of breaking this/leaving me to implement their feature with this new implementation.

@cdoern: PR needs rebase.

@mheon PTAL, all tests passing and I think I avoided any breaking changes in the libpod API. sorry for all of the pings on this but thank you for your help!
LGTM. My concern is addressed but @mheon should do a final review.
/lgtm
